# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.
# Category: kong_no_auth

_format_version: "3.0"

services:
  - name: users-service
    protocol: http
    host: users-upstream
    port: 80
    path: /
    retries: 3
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - api-management
      - users-api

  - name: products-service
    protocol: http
    host: products-upstream
    port: 80
    path: /
    retries: 3
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - api-management
      - products-api

  - name: orders-service
    protocol: http
    host: orders-api.internal.example.com
    port: 8080
    path: /api/v1
    retries: 3
    connect_timeout: 60000
    write_timeout: 60000
    read_timeout: 60000
    tags:
      - api-management
      - orders-api

routes:
  - name: users-route
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
      - PATCH
    paths:
      - /users
      - /users/(.*)
    strip_path: true
    preserve_host: false
    service: users-service
    tags:
      - public-api
      - users-v1

  - name: products-route
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
      - PATCH
    paths:
      - /products
      - /products/(.*)
    strip_path: true
    preserve_host: false
    service: products-service
    tags:
      - public-api
      - products-v1

  - name: orders-route
    protocols:
      - http
      - https
    methods:
      - GET
      - POST
      - PUT
      - DELETE
      - PATCH
    paths:
      - /orders
      - /orders/(.*)
    strip_path: true
    preserve_host: false
    service: orders-service
    tags:
      - public-api
      - orders-v1

upstreams:
  - name: users-upstream
    hash_on: cookie
    hash_fallback_on: ip
    hash_on_cookie: JSESSIONID
    hash_on_cookie_path: /
    slots: 10000
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /health
        type: http
        timeout: 5
        healthy:
          interval: 10
          successes: 3
          http_statuses:
            - 200
        unhealthy:
          interval: 5
          failures: 3
          http_statuses:
            - 500
            - 503
            - 404
      passive:
        type: http
        healthy:
          successes: 5
          http_statuses:
            - 200
        unhealthy:
          failures: 5
          http_statuses:
            - 500
            - 503
    tags:
      - users-backend

  - name: products-upstream
    hash_on: ip
    slots: 10000
    healthchecks:
      active:
        http_path: /status
        type: http
        timeout: 5
        healthy:
          interval: 10
          successes: 3
          http_statuses:
            - 200
        unhealthy:
          interval: 5
          failures: 3
          http_statuses:
            - 5xx
      passive:
        type: http
        healthy:
          successes: 5
          http_statuses:
            - 200
        unhealthy:
          failures: 5
          http_statuses:
            - 5xx
    tags:
      - products-backend

targets:
  - upstream: users-upstream
    target: 10.0.1.10:8000
    weight: 100
    tags:
      - production
      - users-node-1

  - upstream: users-upstream
    target: 10.0.1.11:8000
    weight: 100
    tags:
      - production
      - users-node-2

  - upstream: products-upstream
    target: 10.0.2.20:8001
    weight: 100
    tags:
      - production
      - products-node-1

  - upstream: products-upstream
    target: 10.0.2.21:8001
    weight: 100
    tags:
      - production
      - products-node-2

  - upstream: products-upstream
    target: 10.0.2.22:8001
    weight: 50 # Example of lower weight for a specific instance
    tags:
      - production
      - products-node-3
      - reduced-traffic
